soil_analysis_title: SOIL TEST ANALYSIS - KNOW YOUR SOIL, GROW YOUR BUSINESS
soil_ca_advice_low: Low. Needs calcitic lime or gypsum.
soil_mg_advice_low: Low. Needs dolomitic lime or Epsom salts.
soil_ca_mg_ratio_low: Magnesium is too high relative to calcium. Use calcitic lime.
soil_ca_mg_ratio_high: Calcium is high relative to magnesium. Use dolomitic lime.
soil_business_insight: BUSINESS INSIGHT: Every {{symbol}} 1 invested in soil correction returns {{symbol}} 3-5 in higher yields!
soil_test_yearly: TEST SOIL YEARLY to track improvements and adjust inputs.
calcitic_lime_title: CALCITIC LIME RECOMMENDATION FROM YOUR SOIL TEST
calcitic_lime_need: Based on your soil test, you need {{kg}} kg of calcitic lime per acre.
calcitic_lime_application: Apply 3-4 weeks before planting and incorporate into top 10-15cm soil
calcitic_lime_wait: Wait 1-2 weeks before applying nitrogen fertilizers
calcitic_lime_why_acidic_low_ca: Why: Your pH is {{ph}} (acidic) and your calcium is low ({{ca}} ppm). Calcitic lime fixes both problems!
calcitic_lime_why_acidic: Why: Your pH is {{ph}} (acidic). Calcitic lime will raise pH and add calcium.
calcitic_lime_why_low_ca: Why: Your calcium is low ({{ca}} ppm). Calcitic lime adds calcium without adding magnesium.
calcitic_lime_business_case: BUSINESS CASE: Proper pH can increase nutrient uptake by 30-50%!
soil_test_yearly_reapply: TEST SOIL YEARLY to know when to reapply.

gap_title: GOOD AGRICULTURAL PRACTICES FOR YOUR {{crop}} ENTERPRISE
gap_maize:
Seed: Use 10kg certified maize seed per acre. Varieties: H614, H629, PHB 3253
Land prep: Prepare before rains. Plough twice for fine seedbed
Planting: Plant at onset of rains, 75cm x 25cm spacing, 5cm deep
Weeding: 1st weeding at 2-3 weeks, 2nd at 5-6 weeks
Topdressing: Apply at knee-high stage when soil is moist
Maturity: 3-6 months depending on variety
Yield: 25-40 bags per acre with good management
gap_beans:
Seed: Use 20-24kg certified bean seed per acre. Varieties: Rosecoco, Canadian Wonder
Spacing: 50cm x 10cm pure stand, 2 seeds per hole
Planting: Plant early in season to avoid bean fly
Weeding: Keep field clean, avoid weeding during flowering
Maturity: 3-4 months
Yield: 5-15 bags per acre
gap_finger_millet:
Seed: Use 1.6kg certified finger millet seed per acre. Varieties: Serere, Gulu
Land prep: Prepare land to fine tilth
Planting: Plant at onset of rains, 30cm x 15cm spacing
Weeding: 1st weeding at 2-3 weeks, 2nd at 5-6 weeks
Maturity: 3-4 months
Yield: 8-25 bags per acre
gap_sorghum:
Seed: Use 2.8kg certified sorghum seed per acre. Varieties: Seredo, Serena
Land prep: Prepare land to fine tilth
Planting: Plant at onset of rains, 60cm x 15cm spacing
Weeding: 1st weeding at 2-3 weeks, 2nd at 5-6 weeks
Maturity: 3-5 months
Yield: 7-35 bags per acre
gap_generic:
Use certified, disease-free seed/planting material
Prepare land 2-3 months before planting
Plant with first effective rains
Follow recommended spacing
Weed early and regularly (2-3 times per season)
Harvest at correct maturity
Dry to correct moisture and store properly
gap_remember: REMEMBER: Every practice you do well puts more money in your pocket
disease_management_title: INTEGRATED DISEASE MANAGEMENT FOR YOUR {{crop}} ENTERPRISE
disease_prevention_list:
Use disease-resistant varieties where available
Practice crop rotation (3-4 years) for soil-borne diseases
Ensure proper spacing for air circulation
Avoid working in wet fields to prevent spread
Remove and destroy infected plants immediately
Disinfect tools between fields
disease_control_maize_1:
Maize Streak Virus
Use resistant varieties (WH505, WH403)
Control leaf hoppers with appropriate insecticides
Rouge out infected plants immediately
disease_control_maize_2:
Maize Lethal Necrosis Disease (MLND)
Use certified disease-free seed
Control vectors (aphids, thrips) with yellow sticky traps
Uproot and burn infected plants
Practice 2-3 year crop rotation with non-cereals
disease_control_generic:
General Disease Management Tips
Start with certified disease-free seed/planting material
Practice crop rotation (at least 2-3 years)
Ensure proper spacing for air circulation
Remove and destroy infected plants immediately
Apply preventative fungicides during humid conditions
Consult local agricultural extension for specific recommendations
disease_business_case:
Without control: Yield losses of 30-100% possible
With prevention: Cost {{low}}-{{high}}/acre = SAVE {{saved}}+!
Every {{symbol}} 1 spent on disease prevention returns {{symbol}} 20-50 in saved yield
pest_management_title: INTEGRATED PEST MANAGEMENT (IPM) FOR YOUR {{crop}} ENTERPRISE
pest_prevention_list:
Practice crop rotation with non-host crops
Use resistant varieties where available
Monitor fields weekly for early detection (FREE!)
Conserve natural enemies (ladybirds, spiders, parasitic wasps)
Remove and destroy infected plants immediately
pest_business_calc:
Without control: Loss 40-60% yield = {{lowLoss}}-{{highLoss}} loss/acre
With IPM: Cost {{lowCost}}-{{highCost}} = SAVE {{saved}}+ profit
Every {{symbol}} 1 spent on pest control returns {{symbol}} 30-40 in saved yield
conservation_title: SOIL AND WATER CONSERVATION FOR YOUR {{crop}} ENTERPRISE
conservation_organic_manure: Organic Manure: Continue applying 5-10 tons per acre. It improves soil structure and water holding capacity.
conservation_terracing: Terracing: Excellent for slopes! Reduces soil erosion by up to 80%.
conservation_mulching: Mulching: Retains moisture, reduces weeding. Use crop residues - it's FREE!
conservation_cover_crops: Cover crops: Plant mucuna or dolichos between rows. Fixes 40kg N/acre naturally!
conservation_rainwater: Rainwater harvesting: Build water pans - 1,000m³ pan costs {{amount}}, lasts 10 years.
conservation_contour: Contour farming: On slopes >5% - reduces erosion by 50% and retains water.
conservation_business_case:
Mulching saves 2 weeding rounds = {{mulchingSaved}}/acre saved
Cover crops fix 40kg N/acre = saves {{coverCropsSaved}} fertilizer
Every {{symbol}} 1 invested in conservation returns {{symbol}} 5 in saved inputs and increased yields
business_title: FARMING AS A BUSINESS - MAXIMIZE YOUR PROFIT
business_know_costs_list: Track EVERY input: seeds, fertilizer, labour, transport, bags
business_form_groups_transport:
Shared transport: Save {{saved}}/acre
Collective marketing: Get 10-20% higher prices
business_exponential:
Every additional {{symbol}} 1 input returns {{symbol}} 3-5 profit
Keep investing - more inputs = more profits
business_bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: Farming is a BUSINESS. Make every shilling work for you
financial_advice:
FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT TIPS

{{#if totalInvestment}}Your precision fertilizer investment is {{totalInvestment}} for your entire farm. This targeted approach typically increases yields by 30-50% with an ROI of 200-300%.

{{/if}}GROSS MARGIN CALCULATION
Gross Margin = (Yield x Price) - (Seed + Fertilizer + Labour + Transport + Bags)

CALCULATE YOUR ACTUAL GROSS MARGIN
Use the yield and price you entered during the interview
Add up all your actual costs:
  Seeds: Your seed rate x seed cost
  Fertilizer: Total kg used x price per kg
  Labour: Ploughing + planting + weeding + harvesting
  Transport: Number of bags x transport cost per bag
  Bags: Number of bags x cost per bag

PRO TIPS
Track EVERY cost - know your numbers
Compare your yields with benchmarks for your area
Aim for higher management levels to increase profit
Remember: Every {{symbol}} 1 invested should return {{symbol}} 3-5 profit
TEST SOIL YEARLY to know when adjustments are needed
BUY SIZES THAT MATCH YOUR NEED - don't waste money

THIS IS YOUR BUSINESS. MAKE IT PROFITABLE
qa_varieties_maize:
{{farmerName}}, here are MAIZE VARIETIES for your {{county}} ENTERPRISE:

• H614: High yielding, 4-6 months, resistant to lodging
• H629: Drought tolerant, 3-4 months, good for medium altitudes
• H6213: High protein, 4-5 months, excellent for milling
• PHB 3253: Hybrid, 3-4 months, very high yield potential
• WH505: White grain, 4-5 months, resistant to MSV
• DK 777: Drought tolerant, 3-4 months, consistent performance

Local varieties also available at your agrovet.

qa_fertilizer_maize:
{{farmerName}}, here's your MAIZE FERTILIZER INVESTMENT PLAN:

Planting: Apply 50kg DAP per acre (1 bag of 50kg)
Topdressing: Apply 50kg CAN or 25kg UREA per acre 3-4 weeks after planting
Add 2.5 tons manure per acre for best results

Expected yield: 25-40 bags per acre with good management
qa_fertilizer_beans:
{{farmerName}}, BEANS fix their own nitrogen! Just apply:
Planting: 50kg DAP per acre at planting
Add manure for better yields
qa_fertilizer_tomatoes:
{{farmerName}}, here's your TOMATO FERTILIZER INVESTMENT PLAN:
Planting: 100kg DAP per acre
Topdressing 1: 50kg CAN at transplanting
Topdressing 2: 100kg CAN at flowering
Use foliar feeds for micronutrients
qa_fertilizer_generic: {{farmerName}}, for your {{crop}} ENTERPRISE, apply 50kg DAP at planting and topdress with 50kg CAN 3-4 weeks later. Consider a soil test for precision recommendations - it saves money!
qa_seed_maize:
{{farmerName}}, for your MAIZE ENTERPRISE:
SEED RATE: 10kg per acre (25kg per hectare)
Buy certified seed from agrodealers
Cost: {{costLow}}-{{costHigh}} per kg
qa_seed_beans:
{{farmerName}}, for your BEANS ENTERPRISE:
SEED RATE: 20-24kg per acre (50-60kg per hectare)
Certified seed recommended
qa_seed_finger_millet:
{{farmerName}}, for your FINGER MILLET ENTERPRISE:
SEED RATE: 1.6kg per acre (4kg per hectare)
qa_seed_sorghum:
{{farmerName}}, for your SORGHUM ENTERPRISE:
SEED RATE: 2.8kg per acre (7kg per hectare)
qa_seed_tomatoes:
{{farmerName}}, for your TOMATO ENTERPRISE:
SEED RATE: 60-80g per acre (150-200g per hectare) for nursery
qa_seed_onions:
{{farmerName}}, for your ONION ENTERPRISE:
SEED RATE: 0.7-0.8kg per acre (1.75-2kg per hectare) for nursery
qa_seed_generic: {{farmerName}}, for your {{crop}} ENTERPRISE, use certified seed at recommended rates from your local agrovet.
qa_spacing_maize:
{{farmerName}}, for your MAIZE ENTERPRISE:

75cm between rows x 25cm within rows (1 seed per hole)
OR 75cm x 50cm (2 seeds per hole)
Plant 5cm deep
This gives about 53,000 plants per hectare
More plants = more potential profit!
qa_spacing_beans:
{{farmerName}}, for your BEANS ENTERPRISE:

50cm x 10cm for pure stand (2 seeds per hole)
For intercropping with maize, use 50cm x 15cm between maize rows
qa_spacing_sorghum:
{{farmerName}}, for your SORGHUM ENTERPRISE:

60cm x 15cm (drilled then thinned to 15cm intra-row)
qa_spacing_tomatoes:
{{farmerName}}, for your TOMATO ENTERPRISE:

60-70cm x 40cm (processing)
90cm x 30cm (fresh market)
60cm x 60cm (2 bearing stems)
qa_spacing_cabbages:
{{farmerName}}, for your CABBAGE ENTERPRISE:

60cm x 45cm for medium heads
75cm x 60cm for large heads
qa_spacing_onions:
{{farmerName}}, for your ONION ENTERPRISE:

15cm x 7.5cm. Transplant when pencil-thick
qa_spacing_bananas:
{{farmerName}}, for your BANANA ENTERPRISE:

3m x 3m (short varieties)
3m x 4m (medium)
4m x 4m (tall)
Hole size: 60cm x 60cm x 60cm
qa_spacing_coffee:
{{farmerName}}, for your COFFEE ENTERPRISE:

2.75m x 2.75m for SL28/SL34 (1,300 trees/acre)
2m x 2m for Ruiru 11 (2,500 trees/acre)
qa_spacing_generic: {{farmerName}}, for your {{crop}} ENTERPRISE, follow the recommended spacing for your specific variety. Check with your local agrovet for guidance.
qa_pest_tomatoes:
{{farmerName}}, PROTECT YOUR TOMATO ENTERPRISE:

Whiteflies:
• Cultural: Yellow sticky traps
• Chemical: Decis 2.5EC (10ml/20L) or Confidor 200SL (10ml/20L)

American Bollworm:
• Chemical: Karate 5EC (5ml/20L)
qa_pest_generic: {{farmerName}}, practice integrated pest management for your {{crop}} ENTERPRISE: monitor regularly, use resistant varieties, conserve natural enemies, and apply pesticides only when pest levels reach economic thresholds.
qa_disease_maize:
{{farmerName}}, PROTECT YOUR MAIZE ENTERPRISE:

Maize Lethal Necrosis Disease (MLND):
• Use certified seed, keep field weed-free
• Control vectors with yellow sticky traps
• Uproot and destroy infected plants

Maize streak virus:
• Use resistant varieties, control leaf hoppers

Head smut:
• Use tolerant varieties, improve soil fertility
• Remove and burn infected plants
qa_disease_tomatoes:
{{farmerName}}, PROTECT YOUR TOMATO ENTERPRISE:

Late Blight:
• Spray Ridomil or Mancozeb preventatively
• Ensure good air circulation

Early Blight:
• Use resistant varieties, spray Mancozeb
qa_disease_generic: {{farmerName}}, for your {{crop}} ENTERPRISE, use resistant varieties, ensure good air circulation, remove infected plants, and apply fungicides preventatively during wet conditions.
qa_harvest_maize:
{{farmerName}}, MAXIMIZE YOUR MAIZE ENTERPRISE RETURNS:

Harvest timing: 3-6 months after planting (when husks are brown)
Expected yield: 25-40 (90kg) bags per acre with good management

Drying: Spread on tarpaulin, dry to 13% moisture
Storage: Hermetic bags or metallic silos on raised surfaces
qa_harvest_beans:
{{farmerName}}, MAXIMIZE YOUR BEANS ENTERPRISE RETURNS:

Harvest when pods are dry and rattling
Sun-dry thoroughly before storage
Store in hermetic bags or clean containers
qa_harvest_tomatoes:
{{farmerName}}, MAXIMIZE YOUR TOMATO ENTERPRISE RETURNS:

Pick at color break (green to pink) for distant markets
Fully ripe for local markets
Handle carefully to avoid bruising
qa_harvest_generic: {{farmerName}}, harvest your {{crop}} ENTERPRISE at the right maturity for best quality. Handle carefully to reduce losses.
bags_to_kg_example: If you harvested 60 bags of 90kg, enter 5400 kg
yield_seems_low_detail: {{yield}} kg is very low. Did you mean {{bags}} bags ({{converted}} kg)?
yield_seems_low_confirm: ⚠️ {{yield}} kg seems very low. Did you mean {{bags}} bags? That would be {{converted}} kg. Continue anyway?
yield_too_high_detail: Maximum expected is {{max}} kg. Please verify.
price_per_kg_expected: Expected price for {{crop}} is Ksh 40-80 per kg
price_high_verify: Please verify your price per kg is correct
target_yield_low: Target yield of {{target}} kg seems low. Continue?
ack_actual_yield_kg: Thank you. Your yield is {{answer}} kilograms.
ack_price_per_kg: Thank you. Your price is {{answer}} shillings per kilogram.
ack_target_yield_kg: Thank you. Your target yield is {{answer}} kilograms.
gap_avocados:
Seed: Use certified avocado seedlings. Varieties: Hass, Fuerte, Pinkerton
Spacing: 5m x 5m (174 trees per acre) for standard varieties
Soil: Well-drained, deep soil with pH 5.5-6.5
Planting: Plant at start of rains, dig 60cm x 60cm x 60cm holes
Fertilizer: Apply DAP at planting, CAN annually
Watering: Young trees need regular watering, mature trees are drought-tolerant
Maturity: 3-4 years for grafted trees
Yield: 100-300 kg per tree (17,000-52,000 kg per acre) with good management
chemical_note_tanzania_ban: BANNED in Tanzania (Jan 2026). Check local regulations before use.
chemical_note_lambda_cyhalothrin: Supply tight, prices rising; check availability
chemical_note_dimethoate: Restricted in East Africa; verify local registration
chemical_note_diazinon: May be restricted in some countries; check locally
chemical_note_avaunt: May be discontinued in some regions; check local availability
business_note_early_detection: Early detection saves Ksh 5,000+ per acre!
business_note_resistant_varieties: Resistant varieties cost the same as susceptible ones!
qa_water:
{{farmerName}}, here's how to MAXIMIZE your WATER INVESTMENT:

• Water early morning or late evening to reduce evaporation
• Use drip irrigation where possible to save water (saves 40% on water bills!)
• Mulch around plants to retain soil moisture (FREE!)
• During dry spells, water deeply but less frequently
• For rainfed farming, plant with the first rains
• Consider rainwater harvesting structures - pays for itself in 2 seasons!
qa_margin_roi_medium: 👍 Good, {{farmerName}}! But we can do better.
qa_margin_roi_low: ⚠️ {{farmerName}}, we need to improve this return!
qa_margin_generic:
{{farmerName}}, here's how to calculate your GROSS MARGIN:

Gross Margin = (Yield × Price) - (Seed + Fertilizer + Labour + Transport + Bags)

Track ALL your costs to know your true profit! This is the difference between farming and running a PROFITABLE ENTERPRISE.
qa_business:
{{farmerName}}, FARMING AS A BUSINESS - LISTEN UP! 👂

1. KNOW YOUR COSTS: Track EVERY input - seeds, fertilizer, labour, transport, bags
2. BUY IN BULK: Save 20-30% with larger packs ({{symbol}}2,000-{{symbol}}5,000 saved!)
3. FORM FARMER GROUPS: Bulk purchases, shared transport, collective marketing
4. EXPONENTIAL PHASE: Every 1 {{symbol}} invested returns 3-5 {{symbol}} profit!

💥 PRODUCE MORE WITH LESS, {{farmerName}}!
💰 PUT MORE MONEY IN YOUR POCKET!
🚀 THIS IS YOUR BUSINESS - MAKE IT PROFITABLE!

BOTTOM LINE: You're not just farming, {{farmerName}} - you're running an ENTERPRISE!
gap_onions:
Seed: Use 0.7-0.8kg certified onion seed per acre (for nursery). Varieties: Red Creole, Bombay Red, Texas Grano
Spacing: 15cm x 7.5cm. Transplant when pencil-thick
Soil: Well-drained, fertile soil with pH 6.0-7.0
Fertilizer: Apply DAP at transplanting, CAN at 3-4 weeks
Watering: Keep soil consistently moist
Maturity: 3-4 months
Yield: 8-15 bags per acre with good management
chemical_note_tanzania_ban: BANNED in Tanzania (Jan 2026). Check local regulations before use.
chemical_note_lambda_cyhalothrin: Supply tight, prices rising; check availability
chemical_note_dimethoate: Restricted in East Africa; verify local registration
chemical_note_diazinon: May be restricted in some countries; check locally
chemical_note_avaunt: May be discontinued in some regions; check local availability
business_note_early_detection: Early detection saves Ksh 5,000+ per acre!
business_note_resistant_varieties: Resistant varieties cost the same as susceptible ones!
business_note_raised_beds: Raised beds are FREE prevention for root rots!
business_note_rotation: Crop rotation costs nothing but time!
business_note_traps: Pheromone traps cost Ksh 500/acre - cheaper than spraying!
business_note_harvest_frequency: Frequent harvesting reduces pest pressure by 60%!
business_note_shade_management: Proper shade reduces pest infestation by 40-60%!
gap_rice:
Seed: Use certified rice seed. Varieties: Komboka, IR54, BW196, Basmati
Spacing: 20cm x 20cm (transplanted) or 25cm rows (direct seeded)
Soil: Clay loam soil with good water retention, pH 5.5-6.5
Planting: Transplant 3-4 week seedlings at 2-3 per hill
Water: Maintain 5-10cm water depth during growing season
Fertilizer: Apply DAP at planting, CAN at tillering
Maturity: 3-4 months for early maturing, 5-6 for traditional
Yield: 3,000-6,000 kg per acre with good management
gap_mangoes:
Seed: Use grafted mango seedlings. Varieties: Apple, Kent, Tommy Atkins, Van Dyke
Spacing: 8m x 8m (68 trees per acre) for standard varieties
Soil: Deep, well-drained soil with pH 5.5-7.5
Planting: Plant at start of rains, dig 60cm x 60cm x 60cm holes
Fertilizer: Apply DAP at planting, CAN annually, manure 20kg/tree
Watering: Young trees need regular watering, mature trees drought-tolerant
Pruning: Prune after harvest to maintain shape and air circulation
Maturity: 3-4 years for grafted trees
Yield: 100-300 kg per tree (6,800-20,000 kg per acre) with good management
gap_pineapples:
Seed: Use crowns, slips or suckers from disease-free plants. Varieties: Smooth Cayenne, MD2
Spacing: 30cm x 60cm (double rows) or 45cm x 45cm (single rows) - 10,000-15,000 plants per acre
Soil: Well-drained sandy loam with pH 4.5-5.5
Planting: Plant at start of rains, remove lower leaves, treat with fungicide
Fertilizer: Apply NPK at planting, side-dress every 3 months
Watering: Moderate watering, drought-tolerant once established
Maturity: 18-24 months
Yield: 20,000-40,000 kg per acre with good management
gap_watermelons:
Seed: Use certified hybrid seed. Varieties: Crimson Sweet, Sugar Baby, Charleston Gray
Spacing: 2m x 1m (2,000 plants per acre) for trailing varieties
Soil: Well-drained sandy loam with pH 6.0-7.0
Planting: Plant 2-3 seeds per hill, thin to 1 plant, direct seed or transplant
Fertilizer: Apply DAP at planting, CAN at vining and flowering
Watering: Regular watering during fruit set and development
Pollination: Bees essential - avoid insecticides during flowering
Maturity: 2-3 months
Yield: 10,000-20,000 kg per acre with good management
gap_carrots:
Seed: Use certified carrot seed. Varieties: Nantes, Chantenay, Amsterdam Forcing
Spacing: 30cm rows, thin to 5-8cm between plants (80,000-100,000 plants per acre)
Soil: Deep, loose sandy loam with pH 6.0-6.8 - rocks cause forked roots!
Planting: Direct seed, keep soil moist until germination
Fertilizer: Apply DAP at planting, avoid fresh manure (forked roots)
Watering: Regular even watering - fluctuations cause cracking
Maturity: 2-3 months
Yield: 8,000-15,000 kg per acre with good management
gap_chillies:
Seed: Use certified hybrid seed. Varieties: Cayenne, Bird's Eye, Habanero, Paprika
Spacing: 60cm x 45cm (10,000-12,000 plants per acre)
Soil: Well-drained loam with pH 6.0-7.0
Planting: Transplant 4-6 week seedlings, harden off before planting
Fertilizer: Apply DAP at planting, CAN at flowering, potassium for fruit development
Watering: Regular even watering, mulch to conserve moisture
Staking: Stake tall varieties to prevent lodging
Maturity: 2-3 months for green, 3-4 for ripe
Yield: 5,000-10,000 kg per acre with good management
gap_spinach:
Seed: Use certified spinach seed. Varieties: New Zealand, Fordhook Giant, Bloomsdale
Spacing: 30cm rows, thin to 15cm between plants (40,000-50,000 plants per acre)
Soil: Fertile loam with pH 6.5-7.5
Planting: Direct seed or transplant, keep soil moist
Fertilizer: Apply DAP at planting, CAN after each harvest
Watering: Regular watering, avoid water stress (bolting)
Harvesting: Cut outer leaves, leave growing point
Maturity: 6-8 weeks, multiple harvests
Yield: 8,000-12,000 kg per acre with good management
gap_pigeonpeas:
Seed: Use certified pigeon pea seed. Varieties: ICPL 87091, KAT 60/8
Spacing: 75cm x 30cm (15,000-20,000 plants per acre) for short duration
Soil: Well-drained soils, drought-tolerant
Planting: Direct seed at start of rains, 2-3 seeds per hill, thin to 1
Fertilizer: Apply DAP at planting, no nitrogen needed (fixes own)
Watering: Drought-tolerant, water during flowering for better yield
Maturity: 4-9 months depending on variety
Yield: 800-1,500 kg per acre with good management
gap_bambaranuts:
Seed: Use certified bambara groundnut seed. Varieties: Local landraces
Spacing: 45cm x 20cm (30,000-40,000 plants per acre)
Soil: Well-drained sandy loam, drought-tolerant
Planting: Direct seed at start of rains, 2 seeds per hill
Fertilizer: No nitrogen needed (fixes own), apply phosphorus
Watering: Drought-tolerant, water during flowering for better yield
Maturity: 3-4 months
Yield: 800-1,200 kg per acre with good management
gap_yams:
Seed: Use clean yam setts (seed tubers). Varieties: White yam, Yellow yam, Water yam
Spacing: 1m x 1m (4,000 plants per acre) for standard varieties
Soil: Deep, well-drained loam with high organic matter, pH 5.5-6.5
Planting: Plant setts at start of rains, 5-10cm deep
Staking: Stake vines to improve yield and reduce disease
Fertilizer: Apply DAP at planting, CAN at vining, potassium for tuber development
Watering: Regular watering during tuber formation
Maturity: 7-12 months
Yield: 10,000-20,000 kg per acre with good management
gap_taro:
Seed: Use clean taro cormels. Varieties: Dasheen, Eddoe
Spacing: 60cm x 45cm (12,000-15,000 plants per acre)
Soil: Moist, fertile soil with good drainage, pH 5.5-6.5
Planting: Plant cormels at start of rains, 5-10cm deep
Fertilizer: Apply DAP at planting, CAN at 2-3 months
Watering: Keep soil consistently moist, can grow in wetlands
Maturity: 6-12 months
Yield: 8,000-15,000 kg per acre with good management
gap_okra:
Seed: Use certified okra seed. Varieties: Clemson Spineless, Pusa Sawani
Spacing: 60cm x 30cm (15,000-20,000 plants per acre)
Soil: Well-drained loam with pH 6.0-7.0
Planting: Direct seed or transplant, soak seeds overnight
Fertilizer: Apply DAP at planting, CAN at flowering
Watering: Regular watering during pod development
Harvesting: Harvest every 2-3 days when pods are tender
Maturity: 2-3 months
Yield: 6,000-10,000 kg per acre with good management
gap_tea:
Seed: Use clonal tea cuttings. Varieties: TRFK 31/37, TRFK 306, TRFK 6/8
Spacing: 1.2m x 0.75m (4,500 plants per acre) for high density
Soil: Deep, well-drained acidic soil with pH 4.5-5.5
Planting: Plant at start of rains, maintain 40-50% shade
Fertilizer: Apply NPK 25:5:5 annually, split applications
Pruning: Prune every 3-4 years to maintain plucking table
Plucking: Pluck every 7-14 days, 2 leaves and a bud
Maturity: 3-4 years to first harvest
Yield: 2,000-4,000 kg made tea per acre with good management
gap_macadamia:
Seed: Use grafted macadamia seedlings. Varieties: KRG 1-4, Murang'a 20
Spacing: 8m x 8m (68 trees per acre) for standard varieties
Soil: Deep, well-drained soil with pH 5.5-6.5
Planting: Plant at start of rains, dig 60cm x 60cm x 60cm holes
Fertilizer: Apply NPK at planting, CAN annually, manure 20kg/tree
Watering: Young trees need regular watering, mature trees drought-tolerant
Pruning: Prune to single leader, remove low branches
Maturity: 4-5 years for grafted trees
Yield: 50-100 kg nuts per tree (3,400-6,800 kg per acre) with good management
gap_cocoa:
Seed: Use hybrid cocoa seedlings. Varieties: Upper Amazon hybrids
Spacing: 3m x 3m (484 trees per acre) for standard planting
Soil: Deep, fertile soil with high organic matter, pH 6.0-7.0
Shade: Maintain 40-50% shade using banana or Gliricidia
Planting: Plant at start of rains, dig 60cm x 60cm x 60cm holes
Fertilizer: Apply NPK at planting, CAN annually
Pruning: Prune to maintain shape and air circulation
Harvesting: Harvest pods when ripe (color change)
Maturity: 3-4 years for hybrid trees
Yield: 500-1,500 kg dry beans per acre with good management
nutrient_deficiency_subtitle: Identify problems by looking at your plants
deficiency_question: What symptoms do you see on your plants?
deficiency_symptom_placeholder: e.g., yellow leaves, purple color, burned edges
deficiency_not_found: No matching deficiencies found. Try different symptoms.
deficiency_consult: Consult your local agricultural extension officer for confirmation.
business_note_early_detection: Early detection saves Ksh 5,000+ per acre in yield losses!
qa_default:
{{farmerName}}, thanks for asking about "{{question}}".

Based on your farm data for your {{crop}} ENTERPRISE in {{county}}:

• Follow good agricultural practices to maximize returns
• Maintain soil fertility - it's your biggest asset
• Monitor regularly for pests and diseases (early detection saves {{symbol}}10,000+!)
• Track ALL costs to know your true profit margin

For more specific information to grow your business, ask about: fertilizer, pests, spacing, harvest, seed rate, varieties, or gross margins.

Remember {{farmerName}}, every shilling counts! 🚀
